payload.title: Windows bootstrapper should create paired User and PC Narada Sites
payload.summary: Narada needs an executable Windows bootstrapper that sets up both the Windows user-locus Site and the Windows PC-locus Site coherently. The docs define the split, and sites init partially exists, but there is not yet a single governed command that creates or updates both loci on a Windows machine.
evidence: Current docs define Windows User Site root as %USERPROFILE%/Narada and Windows PC Site root as %ProgramData%/Narada/sites/pc/{site_id}. The CLI has narada sites init with substrate flags, but help does not expose authority-locus or sync-posture flags, and sites list did not discover the actual local Windows Sites. Our current local setup has a User Site at C:/Users/Andrey/Narada and PC-related needs, but no canonical paired bootstrapper.
proposal: Implement a Windows bootstrap command that performs intent -> dry-run plan -> approval/execute -> evidence for paired User and PC Sites. It should create or update the User Site under %USERPROFILE%/Narada, create or update the PC Site under %ProgramData%/Narada/sites/pc/{site_id}, declare authority_locus explicitly, record sync posture for the User Site, install canonical inbox/file-drop/task lifecycle basics, update or emit Site registry records, validate both roots, and produce mutation evidence. The command should support dry-run and should never infer PC identity solely from hostname when explicit config exists.
payload.recommendation: Add a surface such as narada windows bootstrap --user-site --pc-site --dry-run and --execute, or narada sites bootstrap-windows --user current --pc current. Also update sites init to expose the documented --authority-locus and --sync posture flags or document their replacement. Treat this as a priority because it removes repeated manual setup and prevents user/PC authority collapse on Windows.

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'site_config_change' is not implemented yet.
